info.name: Oracle Fusion & VendHQ Integration API
info.description:
Postman collection for all REST and SOAP API endpoints used in the Oracle Fusion ↔ VendHQ ↔ Opencart integration platform.

## Authentication
- **Oracle Fusion (REST & SOAP):** HTTP Basic Authentication — set `fusion_username` and `fusion_password` in the active Postman environment.
- **VendHQ REST API:** Bearer Token — set `vendhq_api_token` in the active Postman environment.
- **Opencart REST API:** API key — set `opencart_api_key` and `opencart_base_url` in the active Postman environment.

## How to use
1. Import this collection into Postman.
2. Import the companion `Integration_Environment.json` environment file and fill in your credentials.
3. Select the imported environment as the active environment.
4. Execute requests in the order shown (SOAP flows must be run in sequence: Create Invoice → Create Receipt → Apply Receipt).

variable.description: Oracle Fusion Cloud hostname prefix (e.g. ehxk-test)
Oracle Fusion Cloud region code (e.g. em2, us2)

item.name: VendHQ REST API
item.description: Endpoints called against the VendHQ POS REST API (v2.0). All requests use Bearer token authentication.

query.description: Pagination cursor — version offset from which to return sales
Filter by sale status: CLOSED, OPEN, LAYBY, VOIDED, REFUNDED

request.description: Fetches a paginated list of sales from VendHQ. Used by VendHQSalesToFusionInvRecIntParallel to retrieve POS transactions for synchronisation into Oracle Fusion.
Retrieves all outlets (store locations) configured in VendHQ. Used to map each sale to its originating outlet and determine Oracle Fusion Business Unit and Org IDs.
Retrieves all products from VendHQ. Also used to look up the VendHQ product that corresponds to a Fusion inventory item during items sync (FusionItemsToVendHQItemsIntegration).

body.raw:
{
  "name": "Sample Product",
  "sku": "SKU-001",
  "handle": "sample-product",
  "description": "Product synchronised from Oracle Fusion SCM",
  "type": "STANDARD",
  "price": 250.00,
  "tax": 5,
  "tax_name": "VAT",
  "tax_included": false,
  "active": true,
  "inventory": [
    {
      "outlet_id": "outlet-uuid-001",
      "count": 10,
      "reorder_point": 2,
      "restock_level": 20
    }
  ]
}

request.description: Creates a new product or updates an existing product in VendHQ. Called by FusionItemsToVendHQItemsIntegration when syncing items from Oracle Fusion SCM.
Retrieves all POS register configurations. Used to look up cash account IDs and bank account IDs needed when building Oracle Fusion receipt requests.
Retrieves customers from VendHQ. Used to match VendHQ customer records with Oracle Fusion customer accounts during the sales integration.

Retrieves all product brands defined in VendHQ. Used by `VendHQBrandService.getVendHQBrands()` to populate the local `VendhqBrandMeta` table and link each VendHQ product to its Oracle Fusion brand equivalent during item sync.
Retrieves all tax configurations from VendHQ. Used by `VendHQTaxService.getVendHQTaxes()` to populate the `VendhqTaxMeta` table and map VendHQ tax rates to Oracle Fusion tax classification codes.

Retrieves all payment types configured in VendHQ (e.g. Cash, Visa, Master Card). The `payment_type_id` on each sale payment is resolved against this list to determine the Fusion `receiptMethodId` and `remittanceBankAccountId` during receipt creation.

response.name: 200 OK – Payment types list

request.description: Fetches a single product record by its VendHQ UUID. Used by `VendHQItemService.getVendHQItemDetail(productId)` to retrieve full product details including inventory counts, brand, and tax rates.

Fetches a single customer record by its VendHQ UUID. Used by `VendHQCustomerService.getVendHQCustomerDetail(customerId)` to resolve customer details when building Oracle Fusion invoice and receipt payloads.

Uploads a product image (JPEG or PNG) to a specific VendHQ product. Used by `VendHQItemService.insertVendHQItemImage(filePath, productId)` after a new product is created in VendHQ to attach the Oracle Fusion item image. The request body must be multipart/form-data with a single `image` field.

item.name: Oracle Fusion REST API
item.description: Oracle Fusion Cloud REST API endpoints consumed by the integration platform. All requests use HTTP Basic Authentication (Base64-encoded username:password).

query.description: Filter items updated on or after this date (ISO 8601). Set last_sync_date in your Postman environment, e.g. 2024-03-01

request.description: Fetches inventory items from Oracle Fusion SCM. Called by FusionItemsToVendHQItemsIntegration to retrieve the current product catalogue and sync it into VendHQ.
Retrieves current on-hand inventory quantities from Oracle Fusion. Called by FusionOnHandQtyFetch to check stock levels before syncing inventory to VendHQ outlets.
Retrieves available-to-promise (ATP) quantities from Oracle Fusion SCM. Used alongside on-hand quantities to determine stock levels to synchronise into VendHQ.
Retrieves all inventory organisations defined in Oracle Fusion. Used to map VendHQ outlet IDs to Fusion Org IDs and Business Unit IDs.

query.description: Filter by org code and transaction date

request.description: Retrieves inventory transactions from Oracle Fusion. Used to track inventory movements and update on-hand quantities in VendHQ.
Retrieves subinventory definitions from Oracle Fusion. Used to identify which subinventory stores the items being synchronised.
Retrieves all Unit of Measure codes from Oracle Fusion. Used to populate the UOM code on invoice lines when creating Fusion invoices from VendHQ sales.

Posts one or more inventory transaction lines to Oracle Fusion SCM to adjust on-hand quantities. Used by `FusionInvTxnService.insertInvTransactions()` after each VendHQ sale is processed; negative `TransactionQuantity` values represent stock issues (sales), positive values represent receipts.

### Key TransactionLine fields
| Field | Example | Notes |
|-------|---------|-------|
| OrganizationName | Ibrahim AL Qurashi - KSA | Inventory org |
| Item | OIL00001 | Fusion item number |
| Subinventory | IBRAHEEM ALQURASHI TAHLIA | Sub-inventory code |
| TransactionType | Miscellaneous issue | Fusion transaction type |
| TransactionUnitOfMeasure | Gram | UOM name |
| TransactionQuantity | -3.0 | Negative = issue, positive = receipt |
| TransactionDate | ISO-8601 datetime | Must include timezone offset |

item.name: Oracle Fusion SOAP Web Services
item.description:
Oracle Fusion Cloud SOAP/JAX-WS endpoints. All requests use HTTP Basic Authentication and send SOAP 1.1 XML envelopes. Replace `{hostname}` and `{region}` with the actual Fusion Cloud instance details.

**Base WSDL pattern:**
`https://{hostname}.fa.{region}.oraclecloud.com/fscmService/{ServiceName}?WSDL`

**Authentication header (add to every request):**
`Authorization: Basic <Base64(username:password)>`

body.raw:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<soapenv:Envelope
    xmlns:soapenv="http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/soap/envelope/"
    xmlns:inv="http://xmlns.oracle.com/apps/financials/receivables/transactions/invoices/invoiceService/">
  <soapenv:Header/>
  <soapenv:Body>
    <inv:createSimpleInvoice>
      <inv:invoiceHeader>
        <!-- Customer Details -->
        <inv:BillToCustomerName>John Doe</inv:BillToCustomerName>
        <inv:BillToAccountNumber>CUST-001</inv:BillToAccountNumber>
        <inv:BillToLocation>AE-DUBAI</inv:BillToLocation>
        <!-- Business Unit / Transaction -->
        <inv:BusinessUnit>Vision Corporation BU</inv:BusinessUnit>
        <inv:TransactionSource>Manual</inv:TransactionSource>
        <inv:TransactionType>PASA CONSULTING SALE</inv:TransactionType>
        <!-- Currency -->
        <inv:InvoiceCurrencyCode>AED</inv:InvoiceCurrencyCode>
        <inv:ConversionRateType>Corporate</inv:ConversionRateType>
        <!-- Payment Terms -->
        <inv:PaymentTermsName>Immediate</inv:PaymentTermsName>
        <!-- Dates -->
        <inv:TransactionDate>2024-03-14</inv:TransactionDate>
        <inv:GlDate>2024-03-14</inv:GlDate>
        <!-- Invoice Lines -->
        <inv:invoiceLine>
          <inv:LineNumber>1</inv:LineNumber>
          <inv:ItemNumber>SKU-001</inv:ItemNumber>
          <inv:Description>Sample Product – Dubai outlet POS sale</inv:Description>
          <inv:Quantity>2</inv:Quantity>
          <inv:UomCode>Ea</inv:UomCode>
          <inv:UnitSellingPrice>250.00</inv:UnitSellingPrice>
          <inv:CurrencyCode>AED</inv:CurrencyCode>
          <inv:SalesOrder>INV-10001</inv:SalesOrder>
          <inv:SalesOrderLine>1</inv:SalesOrderLine>
          <inv:TaxClassificationCode>UAE-VAT-5</inv:TaxClassificationCode>
        </inv:invoiceLine>
        <!-- Discount line (optional) -->
        <inv:invoiceLine>
          <inv:LineNumber>2</inv:LineNumber>
          <inv:MemoLineName>DISCOUNT</inv:MemoLineName>
          <inv:Description>Promotional discount</inv:Description>
          <inv:Quantity>1</inv:Quantity>
          <inv:UomCode>Ea</inv:UomCode>
          <inv:UnitSellingPrice>-25.00</inv:UnitSellingPrice>
          <inv:CurrencyCode>AED</inv:CurrencyCode>
          <inv:SalesOrder>INV-10001</inv:SalesOrder>
          <inv:SalesOrderLine>2</inv:SalesOrderLine>
        </inv:invoiceLine>
      </inv:invoiceHeader>
    </inv:createSimpleInvoice>
  </soapenv:Body>
</soapenv:Envelope>

request.description:
**Purpose:** Creates a Receivables invoice in Oracle Fusion AR for a VendHQ POS sale.

**WSDL:** `{{fusion_base_url}}/fscmService/RecInvoiceService?WSDL`
**Operation:** `createSimpleInvoice`
**Namespace:** `http://xmlns.oracle.com/apps/financials/receivables/transactions/invoices/invoiceService/`
**Mapped by:** `FusionInvoiceMapping.java`

### Key InvoiceHeader fields
| Field | Example | Notes |
|-------|---------|-------|
| BillToCustomerName | John Doe | Customer name |
| BillToAccountNumber | CUST-001 | Customer account in Fusion |
| BusinessUnit | Vision Corporation BU | Fusion BU name |
| TransactionSource | Manual | |
| TransactionType | PASA CONSULTING SALE | |
| InvoiceCurrencyCode | AED | ISO 4217 |
| PaymentTermsName | Immediate | Fetched from CustomerProfileService |
| TransactionDate | 2024-03-14 | VendHQ sale date |

### Invoice Line fields
| Field | Example | Notes |
|-------|---------|-------|
| ItemNumber | SKU-001 | Fusion item number |
| Quantity | 2 | |
| UomCode | Ea | Unit of measure |
| UnitSellingPrice | 250.00 | |
| SalesOrder | INV-10001 | VendHQ invoice number |

body.raw:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<soapenv:Envelope
    xmlns:soapenv="http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/soap/envelope/"
    xmlns:rec="http://xmlns.oracle.com/apps/financials/receivables/receipts/shared/standardReceiptService/commonService/">
  <soapenv:Header/>
  <soapenv:Body>
    <rec:createStandardReceipt>
      <rec:standardReceipt>
        <!-- Currency & Amount -->
        <rec:CurrencyCode>AED</rec:CurrencyCode>
        <rec:Amount>
          <rec:value>550.00</rec:value>
          <rec:currencyCode>AED</rec:currencyCode>
        </rec:Amount>
        <!-- Receipt Identification -->
        <rec:ReceiptNumber>Cash-INV-10001</rec:ReceiptNumber>
        <!-- Date Fields -->
        <rec:ReceiptDate>2024-03-14</rec:ReceiptDate>
        <rec:GlDate>2024-03-14</rec:GlDate>
        <rec:DepositDate>2024-03-14</rec:DepositDate>
        <!-- Payment Method -->
        <rec:ReceiptMethodId>100001</rec:ReceiptMethodId>
        <rec:RemittanceBankAccountId>200001</rec:RemittanceBankAccountId>
        <!-- Customer -->
        <rec:CustomerId>12345</rec:CustomerId>
        <!-- Business Unit -->
        <rec:OrgId>204</rec:OrgId>
      </rec:standardReceipt>
    </rec:createStandardReceipt>
  </soapenv:Body>
</soapenv:Envelope>

request.description:
**Purpose:** Creates a customer receipt (cash or bank) in Oracle Fusion Receivables. Run this **after** creating the invoice.

**WSDL:** `{{fusion_base_url}}/fscmService/StandardReceiptService?WSDL`
**Operation:** `createStandardReceipt`
**Namespace:** `http://xmlns.oracle.com/apps/financials/receivables/receipts/shared/standardReceiptService/commonService/`
**Mapped by:** `FusionStdReceiptMapping.java`

### Key StandardReceipt fields
| Field | Example | Notes |
|-------|---------|-------|
| CurrencyCode | AED | ISO 4217 |
| Amount.value | 550.00 | Total receipt amount |
| ReceiptNumber | Cash-INV-10001 | Format: `{PaymentType}-{InvoiceNumber}` |
| ReceiptDate | 2024-03-14 | VendHQ sale date |
| GlDate | 2024-03-14 | Same as ReceiptDate |
| DepositDate | 2024-03-14 | Same as ReceiptDate |
| ReceiptMethodId | 100001 | Fusion payment method ID |
| RemittanceBankAccountId | 200001 | Cash or bank account ID in Fusion |
| CustomerId | 12345 | Resolved from account number via CustomerProfileService |
| OrgId | 204 | Oracle Fusion Operating Unit ID |

**ReceiptNumber format:**
```
paymentType + "-" + transactionNumber
// e.g. "Cash-abc123" or "Card-abc123"
```

**RemittanceBankAccountId selection:**
- If `FusionReceiptMethod.receiptIsCash == "1"` → use `registerDetails.getCashAccountId()`
- Otherwise → use `registerDetails.getBankAccountId()`

body.raw:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<soapenv:Envelope
    xmlns:soapenv="http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/soap/envelope/"
    xmlns:rec="http://xmlns.oracle.com/apps/financials/receivables/receipts/shared/standardReceiptService/commonService/">
  <soapenv:Header/>
  <soapenv:Body>
    <rec:createApplyReceipt>
      <rec:applyReceipt>
        <!-- Receipt & Invoice Matching -->
        <rec:ReceiptNumber>Cash-INV-10001</rec:ReceiptNumber>
        <rec:TransactionNumber>TXN-20240314-001</rec:TransactionNumber>
        <!-- Amount (retry loop decrements by 0.01 on each failed attempt) -->
        <rec:AmountApplied>550.00</rec:AmountApplied>
        <rec:ReceiptCurrency>AED</rec:ReceiptCurrency>
        <!-- Dates -->
        <rec:AccountingDate>2024-03-14</rec:AccountingDate>
        <rec:ApplicationDate>2024-03-14</rec:ApplicationDate>
        <!-- Source System -->
        <rec:TransactionSource>VendHQ</rec:TransactionSource>
      </rec:applyReceipt>
    </rec:createApplyReceipt>
  </soapenv:Body>
</soapenv:Envelope>

request.description:
**Purpose:** Applies the receipt created in step 2 to the invoice created in step 1, clearing the open AR balance.

**WSDL:** `{{fusion_base_url}}/fscmService/StandardReceiptService?WSDL` *(same service as createStandardReceipt)*
**Operation:** `createApplyReceipt`
**Namespace:** `http://xmlns.oracle.com/apps/financials/receivables/receipts/shared/standardReceiptService/commonService/`
**Mapped by:** `FusionApplyReceiptMapping.java`

### Key ApplyReceipt fields
| Field | Example | Notes |
|-------|---------|-------|
| ReceiptNumber | Cash-INV-10001 | Must match a receipt created by createStandardReceipt |
| TransactionNumber | TXN-20240314-001 | Fusion invoice number returned from createSimpleInvoice |
| AmountApplied | 550.00 | Decremented by 0.01 per retry (up to 50 retries) |
| ReceiptCurrency | AED | Must match the receipt currency |
| AccountingDate | 2024-03-14 | |
| ApplicationDate | 2024-03-14 | |
| TransactionSource | VendHQ | Source system identifier |

**Retry logic (rounding adjustment):**
```java
// Up to 50 retries
applyReceipt.setAmountApplied(
    applyReceipt.getAmountApplied().subtract(BigDecimal.valueOf(0.01)));
```

response.name: 200 OK – Receipt applied
response.body:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<soapenv:Envelope xmlns:soapenv="http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/soap/envelope/">
  <soapenv:Body>
    <createApplyReceiptResponse
        xmlns="http://xmlns.oracle.com/apps/financials/receivables/receipts/shared/standardReceiptService/commonService/">
      <result>
        <Successful>true</Successful>
        <Message>Receipt applied to invoice successfully</Message>
      </result>
    </createApplyReceiptResponse>
  </soapenv:Body>
</soapenv:Envelope>

body.raw:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<soapenv:Envelope
    xmlns:soapenv="http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/soap/envelope/"
    xmlns:misc="http://xmlns.oracle.com/apps/financials/receivables/receipts/shared/miscellaneousReceiptService/commonService/">
  <soapenv:Header/>
  <soapenv:Body>
    <misc:createMiscellaneousReceipt>
      <misc:miscellaneousReceipt>
        <!-- Currency & Amount -->
        <misc:CurrencyCode>AED</misc:CurrencyCode>
        <misc:Amount>
          <misc:value>-0.50</misc:value>
          <misc:currencyCode>AED</misc:currencyCode>
        </misc:Amount>
        <!-- Receipt Identification -->
        <misc:ReceiptNumber>Cash-INV-10001-MISC</misc:ReceiptNumber>
        <!-- Date Fields -->
        <misc:ReceiptDate>2024-03-14</misc:ReceiptDate>
        <misc:GlDate>2024-03-14</misc:GlDate>
        <!-- Payment Method -->
        <misc:ReceiptMethodId>100001</misc:ReceiptMethodId>
        <misc:ReceiptMethodName>Cash</misc:ReceiptMethodName>
        <!-- Account -->
        <misc:BankAccountName>Cash Clearing Account</misc:BankAccountName>
        <!-- Receivable Activity (for posting) -->
        <misc:ReceivableActivityName>Cash Rounding</misc:ReceivableActivityName>
        <!-- Business Unit -->
        <misc:OrgId>204</misc:OrgId>
      </misc:miscellaneousReceipt>
    </misc:createMiscellaneousReceipt>
  </soapenv:Body>
</soapenv:Envelope>

request.description:
**Purpose:** Creates a miscellaneous (non-customer) receipt in Oracle Fusion AR. Used for cash-rounding adjustments and direct postings to a receivable activity account.

**WSDL:** `{{fusion_base_url}}/fscmService/MiscellaneousReceiptService?WSDL`
**Operation:** `createMiscellaneousReceipt`
**Namespace:** `http://xmlns.oracle.com/apps/financials/receivables/receipts/shared/miscellaneousReceiptService/commonService/`
**Mapped by:** `FusionMiscReceiptMapping.java`

### Key MiscellaneousReceipt fields
| Field | Example | Notes |
|-------|---------|-------|
| CurrencyCode | AED | ISO 4217 |
| Amount.value | -0.50 | Negative for cash-rounding debits |
| ReceiptNumber | Cash-INV-10001-MISC | Format: `{PaymentType}-{InvoiceNumber}-MISC` |
| ReceiptMethodId | 100001 | Fusion payment method ID |
| ReceiptMethodName | Cash | Payment method name |
| BankAccountName | Cash Clearing Account | Uses cash account for rounding |
| ReceivableActivityName | Cash Rounding | AR activity to post to |
| OrgId | 204 | Oracle Fusion Operating Unit ID |

body.raw:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<soapenv:Envelope
    xmlns:soapenv="http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/soap/envelope/"
    xmlns:jrn="http://xmlns.oracle.com/apps/financials/generalLedger/journals/desktopEntry/journalImportService/">
  <soapenv:Header/>
  <soapenv:Body>
    <jrn:importJournals>
      <jrn:JournalHeader>
        <!-- Batch identification -->
        <jrn:BatchName>Jan-21: VENDHQ</jrn:BatchName>
        <jrn:BatchDescription>Journal Import: 209354</jrn:BatchDescription>
        <!-- Ledger & period -->
        <jrn:LedgerId>300000001418025</jrn:LedgerId>
        <jrn:AccountingPeriodName>Jan-21</jrn:AccountingPeriodName>
        <!-- Dates & source -->
        <jrn:AccountingDate>2021-01-14</jrn:AccountingDate>
        <jrn:UserSourceName>Vend</jrn:UserSourceName>
        <jrn:UserCategoryName>Vend</jrn:UserCategoryName>
        <jrn:ErrorToSuspenseFlag>false</jrn:ErrorToSuspenseFlag>
        <jrn:SummaryFlag>false</jrn:SummaryFlag>
        <!-- Journal lines -->
        <jrn:JournalLines>
          <jrn:LedgerId>300000001418025</jrn:LedgerId>
          <jrn:PeriodName>Jan-21</jrn:PeriodName>
          <jrn:AccountingDate>2021-01-14</jrn:AccountingDate>
          <jrn:UserJeSourceName>Vend</jrn:UserJeSourceName>
          <jrn:JeCategoryName>Vend</jrn:JeCategoryName>
          <jrn:GroupId>209354</jrn:GroupId>
          <jrn:ChartOfAccountsId>2001</jrn:ChartOfAccountsId>
          <!-- Account segments (10-segment CoA) -->
          <jrn:Segment1>01</jrn:Segment1>
          <jrn:Segment2>5000104</jrn:Segment2>
          <jrn:Segment3>52</jrn:Segment3>
          <jrn:Segment4>0203</jrn:Segment4>
          <jrn:Segment5>00</jrn:Segment5>
          <jrn:Segment6>01</jrn:Segment6>
          <jrn:Segment7>00</jrn:Segment7>
          <jrn:Segment8>00</jrn:Segment8>
          <jrn:Segment9>00</jrn:Segment9>
          <jrn:Segment10>00</jrn:Segment10>
          <!-- Currency & amounts -->
          <jrn:CurrencyCode>AED</jrn:CurrencyCode>
          <jrn:EnteredCrAmount>1580.50</jrn:EnteredCrAmount>
          <jrn:AccountedCr>1580.50</jrn:AccountedCr>
          <jrn:CurrencyConversionType>Corporate</jrn:CurrencyConversionType>
          <jrn:CurrencyConversionDate>2021-01-14</jrn:CurrencyConversionDate>
          <jrn:CurrencyConversionRate>1</jrn:CurrencyConversionRate>
          <jrn:Status>P</jrn:Status>
          <jrn:TaxCode>N</jrn:TaxCode>
          <jrn:TransactionDate>2021-01-14</jrn:TransactionDate>
        </jrn:JournalLines>
      </jrn:JournalHeader>
    </jrn:importJournals>
  </soapenv:Body>
</soapenv:Envelope>

request.description:
**Purpose:** Imports one General Ledger journal batch into Oracle Fusion GL. Called by `FusionJournalClient.saveImportJournal()` after all receipts for a VendHQ sale have been successfully created in AR.

**WSDL:** `{{fusion_base_url}}/finGlDesktopEntry/JournalImportService?WSDL`
**Operation:** `importJournals`
**Namespace:** `http://xmlns.oracle.com/apps/financials/generalLedger/journals/desktopEntry/journalImportService/`
**Client class:** `FusionJournalClient.java` → `saveImportJournal()`

### Key JournalHeader fields
| Field | Example | Notes |
|-------|---------|-------|
| BatchName | Jan-21: VENDHQ | Unique batch identifier |
| LedgerId | 300000001418025 | Fusion GL ledger ID |
| AccountingPeriodName | Jan-21 | GL period name |
| UserSourceName | Vend | Journal source |
| UserCategoryName | Vend | Journal category |

### Key JournalLine fields
| Field | Example | Notes |
|-------|---------|-------|
| Segment1–10 | 01, 5000104, … | 10-segment chart-of-accounts |
| CurrencyCode | AED | ISO 4217 |
| EnteredCrAmount / EnteredDrAmount | 1580.50 | Credit or debit amount |
| GroupId | 209354 | Links to VendHQ invoice number |

**Returns:** the `JeHeaderId` (Long) of the created journal; `null` on failure.

body.raw:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<soapenv:Envelope
    xmlns:soapenv="http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/soap/envelope/"
    xmlns:cus="http://xmlns.oracle.com/apps/financials/receivables/customers/customerProfileService/">
  <soapenv:Header/>
  <soapenv:Body>
    <cus:getActiveCustomerProfile>
      <cus:customerProfile>
        <!-- Customer account number as stored in Oracle Fusion AR -->
        <cus:AccountNumber>ACC-001</cus:AccountNumber>
      </cus:customerProfile>
    </cus:getActiveCustomerProfile>
  </soapenv:Body>
</soapenv:Envelope>

request.description:
**Purpose:** Retrieves the active AR customer profile for a given account number. Used by `FusionCustomerProfileClient.getPaymentTerms()` and `getCustomerAccountId()` to resolve the customer's `paymentTermsName` and `customerId` before creating an invoice.

**WSDL:** `{{fusion_base_url}}/fscmService/ReceivablesCustomerProfileService?WSDL`
**Operation:** `getActiveCustomerProfile`
**Namespace:** `http://xmlns.oracle.com/apps/financials/receivables/customers/customerProfileService/`
**Client class:** `FusionCustomerProfileClient.java`

### Request fields
| Field | Example | Notes |
|-------|---------|-------|
| AccountNumber | ACC-001 | Customer account number in Fusion AR |

### Response fields used by integration
| Field | Usage |
|-------|-------|
| CustomerAccountId | Passed as `customerId` on standard receipt |
| PaymentTerms | Passed as `paymentTermsName` on invoice |

body.raw:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<soapenv:Envelope
    xmlns:soapenv="http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/soap/envelope/"
    xmlns:bak="http://xmlns.oracle.com/apps/financials/payments/shared/bankAccounts/bankAccountServiceV2/">
  <soapenv:Header/>
  <soapenv:Body>
    <bak:findExternalBankAccount>
      <bak:findCriteria>
        <bak:fetchStart>0</bak:fetchStart>
        <bak:fetchSize>1</bak:fetchSize>
        <bak:filter>
          <bak:group>
            <bak:item>
              <!-- Filter by bank account number to resolve remittanceBankAccountId -->
              <bak:attribute>BankAccountNumber</bak:attribute>
              <bak:operator>=</bak:operator>
              <bak:value>1011203</bak:value>
            </bak:item>
          </bak:group>
        </bak:filter>
      </bak:findCriteria>
      <bak:findControl>
        <bak:retrieveAllTranslations>false</bak:retrieveAllTranslations>
      </bak:findControl>
    </bak:findExternalBankAccount>
  </soapenv:Body>
</soapenv:Envelope>

request.description:
**Purpose:** Looks up an external bank account by account number to obtain its `ExternalBankAccountId`. The returned ID is stored as `remittanceBankAccountId` in `FusionReceiptMethod` and passed to `StandardReceiptRequest` / `MiscReceiptRequest` when creating receipts.

**WSDL:** `{{fusion_base_url}}/fscmService/ExternalBankAccountServiceV2?WSDL`
**Operation:** `findExternalBankAccount`
**Namespace:** `http://xmlns.oracle.com/apps/financials/payments/shared/bankAccounts/bankAccountServiceV2/`
**Client class:** `FusionExternalBankClient.java` → `getBankAccountId()`

### FindCriteria fields
| Field | Example | Notes |
|-------|---------|-------|
| attribute | BankAccountNumber | Criterion attribute name |
| operator | = | Comparison operator |
| value | 1011203 | Bank account number to look up |

body.raw:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<soapenv:Envelope
    xmlns:soapenv="http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/soap/envelope/"
    xmlns:itm="http://xmlns.oracle.com/apps/scm/productModel/items/itemServiceV2/">
  <soapenv:Header/>
  <soapenv:Body>
    <itm:findItem>
      <itm:findCriteria>
        <itm:fetchStart>0</itm:fetchStart>
        <itm:fetchSize>1</itm:fetchSize>
        <itm:filter>
          <itm:group>
            <itm:item>
              <!-- Filter by item number to look up localized item description -->
              <itm:attribute>ItemNumber</itm:attribute>
              <itm:operator>=</itm:operator>
              <itm:value>OIL00001</itm:value>
            </itm:item>
          </itm:group>
        </itm:filter>
      </itm:findCriteria>
      <itm:findControl>
        <itm:retrieveAllTranslations>false</itm:retrieveAllTranslations>
      </itm:findControl>
    </itm:findItem>
  </soapenv:Body>
</soapenv:Envelope>

request.description:
**Purpose:** Searches the Oracle Fusion item catalog for a product by item number and returns its localised description. Used by `FusionItemClient.getItemNameTranslation()` during items sync to populate the VendHQ product name with the Arabic / localised translation stored in Fusion.

**WSDL:** `{{fusion_base_url}}/fscmService/ItemServiceV2?WSDL`
**Operation:** `findItem`
**Namespace:** `http://xmlns.oracle.com/apps/scm/productModel/items/itemServiceV2/`
**Client class:** `FusionItemClient.java` → `getItemNameTranslation()`

### FindCriteria fields
| Field | Example | Notes |
|-------|---------|-------|
| attribute | ItemNumber | Criterion attribute name |
| operator | = | Comparison operator |
| value | OIL00001 | Fusion item number to find |

### Response field used by integration
| Field | Usage |
|-------|-------|
| ItemTranslation[0].ItemDescription | Localised product name pushed to VendHQ |

item.name: Opencart REST API
item.description: Endpoints called against the Opencart e-commerce REST API. Authentication is performed via an API key passed as a query parameter or header.

request.description: Retrieves the product catalogue from the Opencart store. Used by the integration to compare Fusion/VendHQ products against the online store inventory.

request.description: Creates a new sales order in the Opencart store. Called during the sales integration flow to propagate VendHQ POS transactions into the online store.
Retrieves customers from the Opencart store. Used to match Opencart customer records with Oracle Fusion accounts and VendHQ customers during integration flows.

request.description: Creates or updates a product in the Opencart store. Used by `OpencartItemService.insertVendHQItems()` to push Oracle Fusion items to the Opencart product catalog during the items sync job. The `X-Oc-Restadmin-Id` header must carry the Opencart REST admin API key.

query.description: ISO date – start of date range (YYYY-MM-DD)
ISO date – end of date range (YYYY-MM-DD)

request.description: Retrieves a list of Opencart orders with their line-item details, filtered by creation date range. Used by `OpencartSalesService.getOpencartSales(from, to)` to fetch e-commerce orders for synchronisation into Oracle Fusion AR as invoices and receipts. Set `order_date_from` and `order_date_to` environment variables before executing this request.
